Title: Glycoalkaloid-Free Starch Potatoes Generated by CRISPR/Cas9-Mediated Mutations of Genes in the Glycoalkaloid Biosynthesis Pathway Enable More Sustainable Uses of By-Products From Starch Production

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts in the pre-cholesterol sterol pathway contributing to cholesterol biosynthesis upstream of SGA formation; mutation redirects flux away from C27 cholesterol toward C28/C29 sterols.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Pre-cholesterol sterol intermediate(s) in the cholesterol branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Cholesterol-pathway sterol intermediate(s); supports cholesterol formation
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis via cholesterol; pre-cholesterol sterol pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key/limiting step for SGA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR/Cas9 mutation of DWF1-L in potato strongly reduced total glycoalkaloid (TGA) levels in leaves and tubers, with field-grown tubers reaching 0.5% of wild type in a representative event. 2. Sterol profiling showed significantly reduced cholesterol and increased C28/C29 sterols such as campesterol and isofucosterol, indicating flux redirection from cholesterol toward other sterol branches. 3. Full or near-full knockout was more effective than partial editing for lowering SGA.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Toxic defense-related specialized metabolites; relevant to plant interactions with pests/pathogens but problematic for food/feed safety.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of DWF1-L lowers cholesterol supply and markedly reduces tuber and leaf SGA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously reported to associate with cultivar differences in tuber SGA level and to be upregulated in tubers before SGA increases after wounding or light exposure.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with tuber SGA levels; inferred relevance in leaves and tubers from mutant phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolomics (UHPLC-MS/MS for SGAs); sterol profiling by GC-TOF-MS; mutation genotyping by HRFA and Sanger sequencing.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; tubers; also starch-production side-stream fibre and protein fractions derived from tubers
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and field conditions; tuber wounding; constant white light exposure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA-free CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; HRFA and Sanger confirmation; UHPLC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; GC-TOF-MS sterol profiling; greenhouse and field phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to create low-SGA starch potatoes enabling safer protein and fibre side-streams; may be combined with other edits, though post-cholesterol targets appear more efficient.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Some events showed reduced plant height and tuber yield, but this was not significantly correlated with SGA reduction and may partly reflect somaclonal variation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also referred to as SSR2. Double mutation with SMO1-L gave stronger reduction than some single pre-cholesterol mutants, suggesting partial functional compensation by paralogs.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Sterol C4 methyl oxidase-like; SMO3 ortholog
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions in the pre-cholesterol sterol pathway contributing to cholesterol biosynthesis before SGA assembly; mutation causes accumulation of early cholesterol precursors and lowers cholesterol.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Early sterol intermediate(s) including cycloartenol/cycloartanol-related substrates in the cholesterol branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Downstream cholesterol-branch sterol intermediate(s) supporting cholesterol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis via cholesterol; pre-cholesterol sterol pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key/limiting step for SGA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SMO1-L mutation reduced SGA levels in leaves and tubers, confirming its involvement in potato cholesterol/SGA biosynthesis. 2. Sterol profiling of SMO1-L mutants showed accumulation of lanosterol, cycloartenol and cycloartanol together with reduced cholesterol, indicating a block in the C27 sterol pathway. 3. Combined mutation of SMO1-L and DWF1-L generally produced stronger TGA reduction than single pre-cholesterol mutations.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Toxic defense-related specialized metabolites; useful for plant defense but detrimental to food/feed safety and by-product valorization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Disrupting SMO1-L lowers cholesterol precursor flux and substantially reduces SGA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously associated with cultivar differences in tuber SGA and induced in tubers prior to SGA increase after wounding/light.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolomics (UHPLC-MS/MS for SGAs); sterol profiling by GC-TOF-MS; mutation genotyping by HRFA and Sanger sequencing.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; tubers; tuber-derived fibre and protein fractions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and field conditions; tuber wounding; constant white light exposure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA-free CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; HRFA and Sanger confirmation; UHPLC-MS/MS; GC-TOF-MS sterol profiling; greenhouse and field phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A validated new target for lowering potato SGAs; can be stacked with DWF1-L, although yield effects require selection of suitable events.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Some mutants showed reduced yield; single representative field event retained only 81% relative yield and showed light-induced TGA increase higher than other strong mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also called SMO3. The study states a key role of SMO1-L in potato SGA biosynthesis is shown here for the first time.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a step in the cholesterol-forming sterol pathway, with lathosterol supported as a substrate in potato; mutation lowers cholesterol supply for SGA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Downstream cholesterol-branch sterol intermediate(s) leading to cholesterol
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis via cholesterol; pre-cholesterol sterol pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key/limiting step for SGA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DWF7-L knockout reduced SGA levels, confirming a role in potato SGA biosynthesis for the first time. 2. Mutants accumulated lophenol and lathosterol and had reduced cholesterol, supporting DWF7-L placement in the cholesterol pathway. 3. DWF7-L mutants also showed lower sitosterol and stigmasterol, suggesting some contribution to the C29 sterol pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-associated toxic specialized metabolites that compromise food/feed safety.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of DWF7-L reduces cholesterol-related sterol flux and lowers SGA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously associated with cultivar differences in tuber SGA and induced in tubers before SGA increase after wounding/light.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolomics (UHPLC-MS/MS for SGAs); sterol profiling by GC-TOF-MS; mutation genotyping by HRFA and Sanger sequencing.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA-free CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; HRFA and Sanger confirmation; UHPLC-MS/MS; GC-TOF-MS sterol profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for mechanistic dissection of cholesterol-to-SGA flux; less emphasized as engineering target because severe yield penalty occurred in the field-tested line.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Field-tested event showed extremely poor yield (3% of wild type), though n.a. metabolite data for that event limit interpretation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also called C5-SD2. The paper explicitly states the key role of DWF7-L in potato SGA biosynthesis is shown here for the first time.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cholesterol-derived steroid intermediate at C16alpha in the post-cholesterol SGA pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Cholesterol-derived steroid intermediate in SGA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate downstream intermediate in target pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key/limiting step; highly decisive target
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. All five full-knockout 16DOX events displayed near absence of total glycoalkaloids in leaves and tubers, including under wounding and light exposure. 2. 16DOX mutants showed the lowest SGA levels among all edited genes, approaching detection limit in tubers. 3. Sterol metabolism was not significantly altered, indicating a specific post-cholesterol role and making 16DOX a particularly efficient engineering target.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-associated toxic specialized metabolites; lowering them improves food/feed safety and side-stream utilization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: 16DOX knockout almost abolishes SGA accumulation in potato leaves, tubers, and tuber-derived protein/fibre fractions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously associated with cultivar differences in tuber SGA level and induced in tubers before SGA increase after wounding or light exposure.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolomics (UHPLC-MS/MS for SGAs); sterol profiling by GC-TOF-MS; mutation genotyping by HRFA and Sanger sequencing.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; tubers; tuber-derived fibre and protein fractions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and field conditions; tuber wounding; constant white light exposure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA-free CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; HRFA and Sanger confirmation; UHPLC-MS/MS across organs and side-stream fractions; GC-TOF-MS sterol profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Best-performing target in this study for generating glycoalkaloid-free starch potatoes and food-grade protein/fibre side-streams, with minimal sterol pathway disturbance.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Some yield reduction observed in tested lines, but low SGA itself was not correlated with reduced yield.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also called GAME11. The paper notes 16DOX was the most efficient single-gene target and appears to be a single-copy-like target in this context.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an oxidation step in the post-cholesterol SGA pathway required for formation of downstream SGA intermediates from cholesterol-derived substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Cholesterol-derived steroid intermediate in SGA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Oxidized downstream steroid intermediate(s) for SGA formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate required for target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key enzyme, but strong reduction required full out-of-frame knockout
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The single full out-of-frame CYP88B1 knockout had almost zero TGA in leaves and tubers, corroborating CYP88B1 as a key SGA enzyme. 2. Partial or in-frame mutants were much less effective, and a field-tested group 2 mutant retained 24.3% of wild-type tuber TGA. 3. Mutant sterol changes were modest, but previous cited work and this study suggest broader metabolic effects may occur.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-associated toxic metabolites affecting food/feed safety.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Complete knockout of CYP88B1 can nearly eliminate SGAs, but incomplete editing gives weaker reduction.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously associated with cultivar differences in tuber SGA and induced before SGA increase after wounding/light.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolomics (UHPLC-MS/MS for SGAs); sterol profiling by GC-TOF-MS; mutation genotyping by HRFA and Sanger sequencing.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and field conditions; tuber wounding; constant white light exposure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA-free CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; HRFA and Sanger confirmation; UHPLC-MS/MS; GC-TOF-MS sterol profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to strongly suppress SGAs if full knockout is achieved, but editing outcome and possible growth penalties make it less straightforward than 16DOX.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: The only full knockout event described showed dwarf phenotype and reduced tuber yield; broader off-target metabolic shifts are possible per cited literature.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also called GAME4 or PGA3. Strong phenotype depended on full out-of-frame knockout, unlike some other targets.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a transamination step in the post-cholesterol SGA pathway contributing to solanidine/SGA formation.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Post-cholesterol steroid intermediate(s) in SGA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Aminated/transaminated steroid intermediate(s) leading to SGAs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate required for target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as important key step, but not as absolute terminal determinant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. All nine TAMiso2 knockout events analyzed showed significantly reduced SGA levels in leaves and tubers. 2. TGA reduction in TAMiso2 mutants was stronger in leaves than in tubers, suggesting organ-dependent complementation by related isoforms. 3. Full-knockout tubers had much lower TGA than wild type but still around 100-fold higher than 16DOX mutants, indicating partial redundancy.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-associated toxic metabolites; reducing them improves food/feed safety.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TAMiso2 knockout substantially reduces SGA accumulation, with stronger effect in leaves than tubers.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously associated with cultivar differences in tuber SGA and induced in tubers before SGA increase after wounding/light.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol (reported in discussion via recent external study for GABA-T2/GAME12)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolomics (UHPLC-MS/MS for SGAs); sterol profiling by GC-TOF-MS; mutation genotyping by HRFA and Sanger sequencing.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; tubers; tuber-derived fibre and protein fractions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and field conditions; tuber wounding; constant white light exposure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA-free CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; HRFA and Sanger confirmation; UHPLC-MS/MS in leaves, tubers, and side-stream fractions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for lowering SGAs while retaining normal morphology in some greenhouse-grown events; organ-specific effects may be exploitable for low-tuber/high-leaf SGA ideotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Field-tested line showed substantial yield reduction; residual tuber SGA likely due to redundancy with other GABA-T isoforms.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also called GAME12 or PGA4. Discussion suggests complementation by other GABA-T isoforms, especially GABA-T4, may explain incomplete tuber SGA suppression.
